messages.content:
No problem, I can appreciate your position.  In fact, I am the DG of an entirely separate area; I had referred you to the appropriate line of business and asked that they support your inquiry.  I believe they’d sent an email to you recently – would recommend you engage them directly through that channel.  Apologies that I’m not able to assist further, but I do not oversee that area and am sure they’re doing their best to support a decision outcome.  Hope it comes in time for your studies,
d
**Dan Anson**
Director General | Directeur général
Intelligence and Investigations Directorate | Direction du renseignement et des enquêtes
Canada Border Services Agency | Agence des services frontaliers du Canada
343-541-7870 (mobile) | daniel.anson@cbsa-asfc.gc.ca
**From:** Yilei Tu (Brunner) <abuzhidaoa8@gmail.com>   
**Sent:** December 9, 2025 10:30 AM  
**To:** Anson, Daniel <Daniel.Anson@cbsa-asfc.gc.ca>  
**Subject:** Re: Request for Completion of My Study Permit Security Screening CA2025-0167467B
| *****ATTENTION*****
This email originated from outside of the Government of Canada.   
**Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and believe the content is safe.**  
To learn more about suspicious emails and how to report them: Handling SPAM or junk emails. 
* * *
Ce courriel provient de l’extérieur du Gouvernement du Canada.   
**Ne cliquez pas sur le lien ni les pièces jointes sauf si vous reconnaissez l’expéditeur et que vous croyez que le contenu est sûr.**  
Pour en savoir plus sur les courriels suspects et comment les signaler: Comment traiter les courriels indésirables. |
| --- |
Dear Mr. Anson,
Sorry to bother you again. I wanted to kindly follow up, as another week has passed and I have not yet received any further update from CBSA regarding my file. May I please ask whether there has been any progress on my case, or whether there is an expected date when I could receive some updates?
I fully understand that, as Director, you may not be directly overseeing the detailed processing of individual files. If possible, would you be able to refer me to the appropriate analyst or officer currently responsible for my case, so that I may inquire about what is currently holding it up? I would be very grateful for any indication of the specific stage or issue that is pending, and I would be more than willing to provide any information that could help move the file forward.
Thank you again for your time and continued assistance. I sincerely appreciate your support.
Best regards,
Yilei Tu
------------------------
He/Him
PhD Student (Incoming)
Dept of Computer Science, University of British Columbia
Vancouver, BC, Canada
https://yileitu.github.io/
**From:** Anson, Daniel <Daniel.Anson@cbsa-asfc.gc.ca>  
**Date:** Wednesday, December 3, 2025 at 01:22  
**To:** Tu Yilei <abuzhidaoa8@gmail.com>  
**Subject:** Re: Request for Completion of My Study Permit Security Screening CA2025-0167467B
Appreciate the message and hope you receive an update or outcome soonest.  I did reach out again so hopefully this helps to move things ahead.  All the best,
D
Dan Anson
CBSA
343-541-7870
> On Dec 2, 2025, at 10:00 AM, Tu Yilei <abuzhidaoa8@gmail.com> wrote:
> | *****ATTENTION*****
> 
> This email originated from outside of the Government of Canada.  
> **Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and believe the content is safe.**  
> To learn more about suspicious emails and how to report them: Handling SPAM or junk emails.
> 
> * * *
> 
> Ce courriel provient de l’extérieur du Gouvernement du Canada.  
> **Ne cliquez pas sur le lien ni les pièces jointes sauf si vous reconnaissez l’expéditeur et que vous croyez que le contenu est sûr.**  
> Pour en savoir plus sur les courriels suspects et comment les signaler: Comment traiter les courriels indésirables. |
> | --- |
> 
> Dear Mr. Anson,
> 
> Thank you very much for your patient and timely reply.
> 
> Almost at the same time you wrote to me, at 12:57 PM yesterday, I received a response from **CINSS PUBLIC / CFSNI PUBLIC (CBSA/ASFC)** <CINSS_PUBLIC-CFSNI_PUBLIC@cbsa-asfc.gc.ca> stating that _“… The CBSA can confirm that work remains ongoing for UCI 1139422560 ...”_ Unfortunately, I recognized this as a boilerplate message, as I previously received an identical reply from **VP Intelligence and Enforcement / VP Renseignement et de l'exécution de la loi (CBSA/ASFC)** <VP_IEB-VP_DGREL@cbsa-asfc.gc.ca> on Oct 27, 2025.
> 
> I fully understand that the CBSA IEB carries a very heavy workload, and with the end of the year and the upcoming Christmas holiday, the pressure must be even greater than usual. Still, I sincerely hope that I might hear some positive news before Christmas. Even if my security screening remains in progress, any concrete update—any small indication of its current stage—would greatly ease the anxiety of waiting. My PhD program has already been deferred by one term, and under the current circumstances I am very worried that I may need to defer a second term or even longer.
> 
> Regardless, I am genuinely grateful that you took the time to personally respond to me. Ever since my study permit was placed under security screening, I have contacted IRCC, CBSA, CSIS, OPC, NSIRA, and several other offices. You are the only person who has personally typed a human reply, rather than sending a boilerplate message or leaving my inquiry unanswered. I truly appreciate that.
> 
> Thank you again for your understanding and support.
> 
> Warm regards,  
> Yilei Tu
> 
> **From:** Anson, Daniel <Daniel.Anson@cbsa-asfc.gc.ca>  
> **Date:** Tuesday, December 2, 2025 at 01:57  
> **To:** Yilei Tu <abuzhidaoa8@gmail.com>  
> **Subject:** Re: Request for Completion of My Study Permit Security Screening CA2025-0167467B
> 
> Thanks for you note, Mr. Tu.  And no need to apologize.  I’ll follow up internally to see if they have made progress or can reach out to ensure you’re connected.  Best regards,
> 
> D
> 
> Dan Anson
> 
> CBSA
> 
> 343-541-7870
>

> > Best regards,  
> > Yilei Tu

> > **Sent:** November 24, 2025 10:04 AM  
> > **To:** Anson, Daniel <Daniel.Anson@cbsa-asfc.gc.ca>  
> > **Subject:** Request for Completion of My Study Permit Security Screening CA2025-0167467B
> > 
> > | *****ATTENTION*****
> > 
> > This email originated from outside of the Government of Canada.  
> > **Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and believe the content is safe.**  
> > To learn more about suspicious emails and how to report them: Handling SPAM or junk emails.
> > 
> > * * *
> > 
> > Ce courriel provient de l’extérieur du Gouvernement du Canada.  
> > **Ne cliquez pas sur le lien ni les pièces jointes sauf si vous reconnaissez l’expéditeur et que vous croyez que le contenu est sûr.**  
> > Pour en savoir plus sur les courriels suspects et comment les signaler: Comment traiter les courriels indésirables. |
> > | --- |
> > 
> > Dear Mr. Anson, Director General of CBSA IEB,
> > 
> > I am writing to sincerely and respectfully request your assistance regarding the prolonged delay in the completion of my security screening of Study Permit application, which is currently being processed by the Security Screening and Refugee Directorate, specifically within the Centre for Immigration National Security Screening, China and Americas section/group.
> > 
> > I am a PhD student joining University of British Columbia, whose academic progress and legal status in Canada now depend entirely on the finalization of this security screening. My file has been under review for a considerable length of time, over 6 months, and this delay has placed me in an extremely difficult position.  Every month of further delay puts my PhD program, funding, and future academic career at risk. My life is on hold because of the security screening.
> > 
> > According to the information visible in my GCMS/CAIPS records:
> > 
> > *   **CSIS has already transmitted its security screening assessment to CBSA on June 11, 2025.**
> >     
> > *   **CBSA’s own SRR/PILLAROTT entries indicate these files have been received.**
> >     
> > 
> > *   Every screening flag in my file—indictment, extradition, military service, litigation, TS information—is marked as “No”, **and nothing suggests any unresolved issues.**
> >     
> > 
> > Given this, I am struggling to understand what additional steps remain or why the file has not moved toward completion. I fully respect the importance of national security processes, but I kindly ask whether my case could be reviewed for any unnecessary delay, administrative bottleneck, or procedural hold.
> > 
> > I deeply appreciate the work IEB does, and I understand the pressures on your branch. I am simply pleading for your help in ensuring that my case can move forward after months of uncertainty. If any additional information from me would help expedite the assessment, I would be grateful to provide it immediately.
> > 
> > *   SSRN: 850149681
> >     
> > *   Case IDs: CA2025-0167467B, ED2025-0167468B, PX2025-0167469B
> >     
> > *   Client Number: CL2025-0167466B
> >     
> > 
> > ·                     UCI: 1139422560
> > 
> > *   Study Permit Application No.: S307197788
> >     
> > 
> > Thank you very much for your time, attention, and understanding. I sincerely hope my file can be finalized and returned to IRCC as soon as possible.
> > 
> > Best regards,
> > 
> > Yilei Tu
> > 
> > ------------------------
> > 
> > He/Him
> > 
> > PhD Student (Incoming)
> > 
> > Dept of Computer Science, University of British Columbia
> > 
> > Vancouver, BC, Canada
  
写一封如泣如诉的回信给Daniel Anson：希望您新年假期过得愉快。我知道直接管Security Screening的DG是**Natasha Alimohamed，您是分管其他领域的DG。但是我给她发了邮件完全没有回复，只有您一直好心地回复我。我现在也不知道怎么办了，我有加拿大的旅游签，且已经四次出入过加拿大了。我实在是匪夷所思CBSA为什么还在调查我的安全问题。现在一月到了，加拿大各大学已经新学期又开学了，这意味着我被迫又要晚一学期入学。我的前途和人生都因为这件事on hold了。如果CBSA觉得我有什么风险点，可以邮件询问甚至面试我，我积极配合予以澄清。我在想进入**Security Screening的visa applicant如果既找不到证据证明他有Security风险，也难以证明他完全没有Security风险，那这个案子就会被一直搁置下去吗？搁置到什么时候才会clear呢？CBSA能conclude我的Security screening case就是送我的最好的新年礼物。我知道这件事不是您的职责范围，您就权当我发发牢骚吧，您可以也完全没必要回复。祝您新的一年工作顺利也祝愿加拿大越来越好

Subject: Continued request for guidance on my study permit security screening file

Dear Mr. Anson,  

Happy New Year. I hope you and your family had a peaceful and restful holiday season.

First, thank you again for your kindness and professionalism throughout this process. I understand from your last email that you are the Director General of a different area, and that the DG responsible for the Security Screening line of business is **Ms. Natasha Alimohamed**. I did email her directly, but unfortunately I have not received any reply at all. At this point, you have been the only person at CBSA who has consistently provided a real, human response—and I am truly grateful for that.

I’m writing today not to put responsibility on you (I know this is not your file or your mandate), but because I honestly don’t know what else I can do.

It is now January, and Canadian universities have already started the new term. This effectively means I am being forced to defer yet another semester. My PhD plans, funding timeline, and life arrangements are all **on hold** solely due to the ongoing security screening.

What confuses me most is that I already hold a valid Canadian tourist visa, and I have entered and exited Canada **four times** without issue. Under these circumstances, it feels genuinely hard to understand why my study permit security screening remains unresolved for so long, and what additional concern could still be under investigation.

If CBSA believes there is any specific risk factor or uncertainty in my background, I would sincerely welcome the opportunity to clarify it. If helpful, officers may email me questions or even conduct an interview at any time—I will actively cooperate and provide whatever documentation or explanation is needed.

With respect, I also cannot help but wonder about the process itself: if a visa applicant is placed into Security Screening, and the authorities cannot find evidence confirming a security risk, but also feel unable to “prove” the absence of any possible risk—does the case then remain indefinitely pending? If not, what is the mechanism or threshold for concluding the screening, and how long can it reasonably remain “ongoing” before it is cleared?

At this point, the best New Year’s gift CBSA could give me would be simply to **conclude** my security screening and return an outcome to IRCC, so my academic future can move forward.

Again, I understand this is outside your responsibilities—please consider this message as me “venting” in desperation. You absolutely do not need to respond. I just didn’t know where else to express this, given that every other channel has been silent or purely boilerplate.

Wishing you a successful year ahead, and wishing Canada continued prosperity and strength.

Sincerely,  
Yilei Tu  
UCI: 1139422560  
SSRN: 850149681  
Case IDs: CA2025-0167467B / ED2025-0167468B / PX2025-0167469B  
Study Permit Application No.: S307197788
